title: Consul Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and configurations for managing Consul services.

commands.description: Start a Consul agent in development mode
commands.scenario: Use when you need to quickly start a Consul agent for testing purposes.

commands.description: List all members in the Consul cluster
commands.scenario: Use to check the status and details of nodes in your Consul cluster.

commands.description: Store a key-value pair in Consul's KV store
commands.scenario: Use to save configuration data or other information in Consul's key-value store.

commands.description: Retrieve a value from Consul's KV store
commands.scenario: Use to fetch configuration data stored in Consul's key-value store.

commands.description: Delete a key-value pair from Consul's KV store
commands.scenario: Use to remove configuration data from Consul's key-value store.

commands.description: List all services registered with the Consul catalog
commands.scenario: Use to view all services currently registered in the Consul catalog.

commands.description: List all nodes registered with the Consul catalog
commands.scenario: Use to view all nodes currently registered in the Consul catalog.

commands.description: Acquire a distributed lock with Consul
commands.scenario: Use to synchronize processes across distributed systems using Consul locks.

commands.description: List the peers in the Consul Raft configuration
commands.scenario: Use to view the current Raft peer configuration in a Consul cluster.

commands.description: Remove a peer from the Consul Raft configuration
commands.scenario: Use to remove a failed or decommissioned peer from the Raft configuration.

commands.description: Join a node to a Consul cluster
commands.scenario: Use to add a new node to an existing Consul cluster.
Use to safely remove a node from a Consul cluster without causing disruptions.

commands.description: Stream logs from a Consul agent
commands.scenario: Use to monitor logs from a Consul agent for troubleshooting purposes.

commands.description: Execute a command on a remote Consul node
commands.scenario: Use to run a command across nodes in a Consul cluster for management tasks.
